title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Periodontal Disease
intro: Herbal teas for periodontal disease are a natural remedy used to prevent and treat the bacterial infection and inflammation associated with the condition. These teas contain medicinal properties that can help reduce the growth of bacteria, prevent further inflammation, and promote healing in the gums. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat periodontal disease include peppermint tea, which has antibacterial properties, chamomile tea, which can help reduce inflammation, and eucalyptus tea, which has antiseptic properties that can help fight infection. Additionally, other herbal teas such as sage, rosemary, and green tea have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can also aid in the treatment of periodontal disease.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat periodontal disease, an inflammatory condition affecting the gums and teeth. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation, prevent infection, and promote healing of the affected areas. Echinacea purpurea teas contain bioactive constituents such as alkylamides, caffeic acid, and chicoric acid, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects, contributing to their therapeutic benefits. By consuming Echinacea purpurea teas, individuals can potentially reduce the severity of periodontal disease symptoms, prevent further progression of the disease, and promote overall oral health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 cup of fresh mint leaves, and 1 quart of water.
Combine Echinacea flowers and mint leaves in a heat-proof cup.
Pour boiling water over the flowers and mint. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, for periodontal disease relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat periodontal disease, but they may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with other medications, including blood thinners, diabetes medications, and certain antibiotics. To use Echinacea purpurea teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose, monitor your body's response, and avoid taking them for extended periods, as this may lead to overstimulation of the immune system and increased risk of liver toxicity.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of dried Echinacea purpurea in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, after meals, for 2-3 weeks.
Continue treatment for 3-6 months for optimal results.
Combine with regular dental check-ups and good oral hygiene practices.
Consult a dentist or healthcare professional before starting treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat periodontal disease, a bacterial infection of the gums that can lead to tooth loss. The antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation, particularly its high content of rosmarinic acid, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, help to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal disease. By reducing inflammation and preventing the formation of plaque, Salvia officinalis teas help to treat periodontal disease by promoting a healthy oral environment. The benefits of using Salvia officinalis teas to treat periodontal disease include reduced risk of tooth loss, improved gum health, and a decrease in the need for surgical interventions.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and flowers. Rinse them with water.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to a heat-resistant cup. Pour in the Salvia officinalis mixture.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid with a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea for taste. Stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for periodontal disease relief. Store any leftover tea in the fridge.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Dentistry journal", Salvia officinalis teas for periodontal disease were found to provide anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidant, direct, and indirect anti-bacterial actions through inhibition of bacterial defence against phagocytes.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat periodontal disease due to their antimicrobial properties, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. To use Salvia officinalis teas safely, take precautions to avoid excessive consumption, which can lead to dizziness, headaches, and increased heart rate, and also be aware of potential cross-reactivity with other herbs in the mint family.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Salvia officinalis tea 3 times a day.
Use 2 tablespoons of dried Salvia officinalis herb per cup.
Steep the herb in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Gargle with the tea 2 times a day for 2 minutes each time.
Continue usage for 6-8 weeks for noticeable results.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizome of the ginger plant, have been studied for their potential in treating periodontal disease, a bacterial infection of the gums and bone that support the teeth. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of Zingiber officinale teas help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth, making it an effective herbal preparation in treating this ailment. Bioactive constituents such as gingerols and shogaols in Zingiber officinale teas have been found to exhibit potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, contributing to their therapeutic effects. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat periodontal disease include reduced inflammation, improved oral health, and a reduced risk of complications such as tooth loss and bone destruction.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Zingiber officinale (ginger) roots.
Cut the ginger roots into small pieces and place them in a saucepan.
Add 2 cups of water to the saucepan and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink 1 cup of the ginger tea, 2-3 times a day, to help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of periodontal disease.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of clinical and diagnostic research : JCDR", Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger, were found to have an antimicrobial property with a zone of inhibition of 25mm against oral microorganisms causing periodontal disease.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat periodontal disease, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness due to its active compounds like gingerols and shogaols. Precautions should be taken when using Zingiber officinale teas to treat periodontal disease, including not consuming it in excess, especially for pregnant women and individuals with bleeding disorders, as it may exacerbate these conditions.
preparation_usage: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root powder.
Mix 1 teaspoon of powder with 1 cup of warm water.
Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2 times a day.
Use Zingiber officinale tea for 3-6 months for periodontal disease.
Consult a dentist for regular check-ups during treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been traditionally used to treat periodontal disease, a chronic bacterial infection that affects the gums and bone supporting the teeth. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of Curcuma longa teas help to reduce inflammation and prevent the progression of the disease. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis and Aggregatibacter actinomycetemcomitans. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat periodontal disease include reduced inflammation, improved gum health, and prevention of tooth loss.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried turmeric roots (Curcuma longa) and 1 cup of water.
Boil the water in a pot and add the turmeric roots.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the turmeric roots. Let it cool.
Drink 1 cup of the turmeric tea, 2-3 times a day, to help prevent periodontal disease.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of periodontal research", Curcuma longa teas for periodontal disease may be effective in reducing bone loss and modulating inflammatory mediators, as natural curcumin associated with insulin reduced the diabetes-induced loss of supporting alveolar bone and promoted favorable modulation on osteo-immune-inflammatory mediators in experimental periodontitis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be used to treat periodontal disease, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high polyphenol content, which can also interact with certain medications. Precautions should be taken when using Curcuma longa teas, including starting with a low dose, avoiding it during pregnancy and breastfeeding, and monitoring blood sugar levels in individuals with diabetes.
preparation_usage: Consult a dentist for periodontal disease diagnosis and treatment plan.
Drink 1 cup of Curcuma longa tea, 2 times a day, for 2 weeks.
Use 1 teaspoon of Curcuma longa powder in 1 cup of boiling water.
Repeat the treatment for 2-3 months, under dentist's supervision.
Monitor progress and adjust treatment plan as advised by dentist.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat periodontal disease due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce gum inflammation and prevent tissue damage. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal disease, thereby reducing the severity of the condition. The anti-inflammatory properties of Ginkgo biloba teas also help to reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease, promoting a healthier oral environment. By consuming Ginkgo biloba teas, individuals can benefit from reduced gum inflammation, improved gum health, and a lower risk of tooth loss associated with periodontal disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the Ginkgo biloba leaves in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the Ginkgo biloba leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the leaves.
Drink 1 cup of the Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day for periodontal disease relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be considered for treating periodontal disease due to their potential to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the gums, but users may experience side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, or allergic reactions in rare cases. To use Ginkgo biloba teas safely, one should be cautious of potential interactions with medications and monitor their blood pressure, as high doses may lower it, and avoid consuming it before surgery due to its blood-thinning properties.
preparation_usage: Consult a dentist to confirm periodontal disease diagnosis
Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea, 2 times a day
Take 250-500 mg of Ginkgo biloba extract, 3 times a day
Maintain good oral hygiene through regular brushing and flossing
Monitor gum health and adjust treatment plan with dentist guidance

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been used to treat periodontal disease due to their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which can cause periodontal disease. The bioactive constituents of Eucalyptus globulus teas, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reduce oxidative stress. Regular consumption of Eucalyptus globulus teas has been shown to improve gum health, reduce plaque and gingivitis, and prevent tooth loss, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for the treatment of periodontal disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser.
Rinse the Eucalyptus leaves with cold water and pat them dry with a paper towel.
Add the Eucalyptus leaves to the tea infuser and place it in a cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Eucalyptus leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation and fight periodontal disease.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be beneficial in treating periodontal disease due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, however, possible side effects include allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interaction with other medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions should be taken when using Eucalyptus globulus teas to treat periodontal disease, such as starting with low doses, avoiding it if pregnant or breastfeeding, and monitoring for signs of excessive bleeding or bruising due to its potential to affect blood clotting.
preparation_usage: Rinse mouth with 10% Eucalyptus globulus tea for 30 seconds.
Swish tea around gums for 1 minute, 2 times a day.
Use 2 tablespoons of tea for each swish, in the morning and night.
Drink 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea, 2 times a day.
Continue for 6-12 weeks or as directed by a dentist or doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, derived from the leaves of the rosemary plant, have been traditionally used to treat periodontal disease due to their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of rosemary tea, including carnosic acid, ursolic acid, and rosmarinic acid, help to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that can lead to periodontal disease. The antimicrobial properties of rosemary tea help to prevent the formation of plaque and tartar, which are major contributors to periodontal disease, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The regular consumption of rosemary tea has been shown to be beneficial in treating periodontal disease, promoting healthy gums and preventing the progression of the disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves or 2 teaspoons of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it starts boiling.
Add the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea infuser or a fine-mesh sieve and discard the leaves.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate symptoms of periodontal disease.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "PloS one", Rosmarinus officinalis teas for periodontal disease showed the highest antibacterial activity with a minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) range of 0.08-5.00 mg ml-1.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used as a complementary treatment for periodontal disease, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, such as nausea and diarrhea, due to its potential to interact with other medications and exacerbate existing digestive issues. To minimize potential side effects, it is essential to consume Rosmarinus officinalis teas in moderation, ideally 1-2 cups per day, and to avoid using it in combination with other herbal preparations that may have synergistic or antagonistic effects, such as ginkgo biloba or blood thinners.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 tablespoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis in 1 cup of boiling water.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, after meals and before bedtime.
Use 1-2 tablespoons of the tea 2-3 times a week as a mouthwash.
Swish the tea in your mouth for 30 seconds before spitting it out.
Continue usage for 3-6 months for noticeable improvement in periodontal health.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the roots of the licorice plant, have been traditionally used to treat periodontal disease due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat periodontal disease by reducing inflammation and preventing the formation of plaque, thereby promoting healthy gums and preventing tooth loss. The bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to exhibit these beneficial properties, thereby helping to treat periodontal disease. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat periodontal disease include reduced inflammation, improved gum health, and a lower risk of tooth loss, making it a promising natural remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root
Combine the Glycyrrhiza glabra root with 4 cups of water in a saucepan
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes
Strain the mixture into a cup and add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional)
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for periodontal disease relief
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of International Society of Preventive & Community Dentistry, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been shown to effectively inhibit primary plaque colonizers and periodontal pathogens, making them a useful treatment for periodontal diseases.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be effective in treating periodontal disease due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, but they may cause side effects such as electrolyte imbalances, potassium deficiency, and increased blood pressure. To use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas safely, it is essential to drink them in moderation, monitor your body's response, and avoid consuming them if you have underlying kidney or heart conditions, as excessive consumption can exacerbate these conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, derived from the herb thyme, have been traditionally used to treat periodontal disease due to its antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that contribute to the disease. The bioactive constituents of thyme, including thymol and carvacrol, exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, thereby helping to alleviate symptoms of periodontal disease such as redness, swelling, and pain. Thymus vulgaris teas have been found to be effective in reducing plaque formation, preventing tooth decay, and promoting healing in the gums, ultimately leading to improved oral health and reduced risk of periodontal disease. The benefits of using Thymus vulgaris teas to treat periodontal disease include reduced inflammation, improved gum health, and a lower risk of complications such as tooth loss and systemic infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves or 2 teaspoons of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Thymus vulgaris leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the Thymus vulgaris leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, preferably after meals, for best results.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Thymus vulgaris teas, along with other sources such as cranberries and tea extracts (green or black), have shown efficiency in alleviating periodontal disease.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be effective in treating periodontal disease due to its antimicrobial properties, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications, particularly blood thinners and diabetes medications. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose, monitor liver and kidney function, and avoid using Thymus vulgaris teas in combination with other herbal preparations or medications that may exacerbate its effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Hydrastis canadensis teas, also known as goldenseal, have been traditionally used to treat periodontal disease due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents, including berberine and hydrastine, in these teas help to reduce bacterial growth and inflammation in the gums, thereby preventing further progression of the disease. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects of Hydrastis canadensis teas make them beneficial in treating periodontal disease by reducing plaque formation, preventing tissue damage, and promoting healing. Regular consumption of Hydrastis canadensis teas may also help to reduce bad breath, gum bleeding, and improve overall oral health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Hydrastis canadensis roots.
Chop the roots into small pieces and add 1 tablespoon of chopped roots to 1 cup of boiling water.
Reduce heat to low and let it simmer for 5-10 minutes. Strain the mixture to remove the roots.
Discard the roots and add honey or lemon to taste if desired. You can also add 1 cup of boiling water to make a stronger tea.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, preferably before meals, to help with periodontal disease.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Hydrastis canadensis teas are not specifically mentioned as effective in preventing or treating periodontal disease, but other teas, particularly green and black tea extracts, were mentioned as showing potential in alleviating the condition.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hydrastis canadensis teas can be used to treat periodontal disease due to its antimicrobial properties, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation to treat periodontal disease, including monitoring blood pressure and avoiding excessive consumption, as it may cause blood pressure to drop.

preparations.herb_desc: Syzygium aromaticum teas have been studied for their potential to treat periodontal disease, a bacterial infection that causes inflammation and damage to the gums and teeth. The properties of these teas, including their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, help to combat the bacterial infection and reduce inflammation, making them a promising natural remedy for this ailment. The bioactive constituents of Syzygium aromaticum teas, such as eugenol and cinnamaldehyde, have been shown to inhibit the growth of periodontal pathogens and reduce the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thereby promoting healing and reducing the risk of disease progression. Regular consumption of Syzygium aromaticum teas may help to prevent the onset of periodontal disease, reduce symptoms, and promote overall oral health, making them a valuable addition to a preventative oral care routine.
preparation_procedure: Get 2 cups of water and boil it in a pot.
Take 2 tablespoons of dried Syzygium aromaticum, also known as cloves, and add it to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes to release the flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool down for a few minutes.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help reduce bacteria and inflammation associated with periodontal disease.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Nutrients, Syzygium aromaticum teas, which contain flavan-3-ols and proanthocyanidins, have been identified as a promising source in the prevention and alleviation of periodontal disease.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Syzygium aromaticum teas can be an effective treatment for periodontal disease, but they may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, stomach upset, and mouth irritation due to the essential oils present in the tea. To use these teas safely, it is essential to start with a low concentration and gradually increase the amount, avoid consuming them before bedtime to prevent sleep disturbances, and practice good oral hygiene to minimize the risk of further irritation.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas have been traditionally used to treat periodontal disease, an ailment characterized by inflammation and infection of the gums and supporting tissues of the teeth. The properties of this herbal preparation, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, help to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of harmful bacteria that contribute to this disease. The bioactive constituents of Camellia sinensis teas, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, are responsible for these effects, which help to treat periodontal disease by reducing plaque formation, preventing the attachment of bacteria to tooth surfaces, and promoting healing of the gums. The benefits of using Camellia sinensis teas to treat periodontal disease include reduced inflammation, improved oral health, and a lower risk of developing more severe forms of this disease.
preparation_procedure: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Camellia sinensis leaves into a cup.
Heat 1 cup of water to 212°F (100°C) and let it cool for 2 minutes.
Pour the heated water over the Camellia sinensis leaves and let it steep for 3-5 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup to remove the leaves. Discard the leaves.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, after meals, to help manage periodontal disease.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Camellia sinensis teas for periodontal disease demonstrated positive results in reducing both the plaque index (PI) and gingival index (GI) in the form of mouthwash, toothpaste, and gel.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be used to treat periodontal disease due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions to take when using Camellia sinensis teas for this purpose include being cautious when consuming it in large quantities, especially for people with sensitive stomachs, and avoiding its use during pregnancy and breastfeeding due to limited research on its effects on these conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, have been traditionally used to treat periodontal disease due to their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including cinnamaldehyde, cinnamyl acetate, and eugenol, help to reduce inflammation, prevent the growth of bacteria, and protect against oxidative stress, thereby treating the ailment. The benefits of Cinnamomum verum teas in treating periodontal disease include reduced plaque formation, improved gum health, and enhanced wound healing, making it a promising natural remedy for this condition. The use of Cinnamomum verum teas is thought to be effective in treating periodontal disease due to its ability to modulate the immune response and prevent the progression of the disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cinnamomum verum leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Cinnamomum verum leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea for taste and sweetness.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from periodontal disease symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Nutrients, Cinnamomum verum teas, which contain flavan-3-ols and proanthocyanidins, have shown immunomodulatory and antibacterial effects that may alleviate periodontal disease.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum verum teas can be used to treat periodontal disease, but they may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, including nausea and diarrhea, due to the high concentration of cinnamaldehyde, a compound that can irritate the stomach lining. Precautions when using Cinnamomum verum teas for this purpose include avoiding consumption in large quantities, as excessive intake may lead to interactions with other medications and exacerbate certain health conditions, such as bleeding disorders or low blood sugar.

preparations.herb_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia teas, also known as tea tree oil tea, have been traditionally used to treat periodontal disease due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as cineole and terpinen-4-ol, which help to reduce the growth of bacteria and inflammation in the gums. By reducing the bacterial load and inflammation, Melaleuca alternifolia teas help to treat periodontal disease by promoting a healthy environment for the gums and preventing further damage. The benefits of using Melaleuca alternifolia teas to treat periodontal disease include reduced symptoms, improved gum health, and a lower risk of complications such as tooth loss.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melaleuca alternifolia leaves and 1 cup of water.
Heat the water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Add the fresh Melaleuca alternifolia leaves to the boiling water and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves. Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help reduce inflammation and fight periodontal disease.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Melaleuca alternifolia teas are not specifically mentioned in relation to periodontal disease prevention or treatment, but tea extracts (green or black) were mentioned as a potential source of flavan-3-ols with immunomodulatory effects for periodontal disease.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melaleuca alternifolia teas can be used to treat periodontal disease, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal upset, headaches, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include being cautious with its interaction with other medications, such as blood thinners, and monitoring for signs of over-sensitivity or intolerance, especially in cases of prolonged use or high concentrations.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat periodontal disease due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce swelling and prevent bacterial growth in the gums. The bioactive constituents, including quinine and quinidine, have been found to exhibit potent antibacterial effects that help to combat the underlying causes of periodontal disease. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial overgrowth, Cinchona officinalis teas can help to alleviate symptoms and promote the healing of periodontal tissues. Regular consumption of Cinchona officinalis teas has been found to improve gum health and reduce the risk of periodontal disease, making it a valuable natural remedy for this common oral health condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Cinchona officinalis bark, 1 cup of boiling water, and a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the Cinchona bark in the cup and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the bark.
Add honey to taste to the tea, if desired. Stir well to dissolve the honey.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for periodontal health and dental care.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Nutrients, Cinchona officinalis teas are not specifically mentioned in the provided study as a treatment for periodontal disease, however, the study suggests that teas, including green or black tea extracts, may be beneficial in alleviating the condition.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used as a potential alternative treatment for periodontal disease, but may cause side effects such as increased heart rate, nausea, and stomach upset due to its high quinine content. Precautions should be taken when using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat periodontal disease, including monitoring blood pressure and kidney function, as well as avoiding excessive consumption, especially in individuals with pre-existing cardiovascular conditions.

By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, Melissa officinalis teas help to alleviate symptoms of periodontal disease, such as redness and swelling of the gums, and prevent further damage to the teeth and surrounding tissues. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat periodontal disease include reduced risk of tooth loss, improved oral health, and a decrease in the need for surgical interventions.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to a heat-resistant cup.
Add the Melissa officinalis leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help with periodontal disease.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Nutrients, Melissa officinalis teas have not been specifically mentioned as a treatment for periodontal disease in the provided study, but the study suggests that tea extracts, such as green or black tea, have shown efficiency in alleviating periodontal disease.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be used as a potential treatment for periodontal disease, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Melissa officinalis teas to treat periodontal disease, it is recommended to take precautions such as starting with small doses, monitoring blood sugar levels, and avoiding use during pregnancy and breastfeeding due to limited research on its effects on the fetus or baby.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, also known as English lavender tea, have been studied for their potential to treat periodontal disease due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including linalool and linalyl acetate, help to reduce the growth of bacteria and inflammation associated with periodontal disease. By reducing the bacterial load and inflammation, Lavandula angustifolia teas may help to prevent the progression of periodontal disease and promote a healthy oral environment. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat periodontal disease include reduced symptoms, improved oral health, and a reduced risk of complications such as tooth loss.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers or 1 tablespoon of dried flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils.
Add the Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the flowers. Let the tea cool down.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage periodontal disease symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat periodontal disease, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners. Precautions to take when using Lavandula angustifolia teas include being cautious of potential interactions with other herbal preparations, and avoiding consumption if pregnant or breastfeeding, as the effects on the fetus or infant are not well understood.

preparations.herb_desc: Origanum vulgare teas, also known as oregano tea, have been traditionally used to treat periodontal disease due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents, such as carvacrol and thymol, exhibit potent antibacterial activity against the bacteria responsible for periodontal disease, including Porphyromonas gingivalis and Aggregatibacter actinomycetemcomitans. This herbal preparation helps to treat periodontal disease by reducing plaque formation, preventing gum inflammation, and promoting wound healing. The benefits of using Origanum vulgare teas to treat periodontal disease include reduced symptoms, improved oral health, and prevention of disease progression.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Origanum vulgare leaves and flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 2 tablespoons of the Origanum vulgare leaves and flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain and drink the tea 2 times a day for optimal results.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Nutrients, Origanum vulgare teas for periodontal disease have not been mentioned in the study, but it mentions tea extracts as a promising source of flavan-3-ols and proanthocyanidins for the prevention and alleviation of periodontal disease.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Origanum vulgare teas can be used to treat periodontal disease, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, such as stomach cramps and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of volatile oils and tannins. Precautions should be taken when using Origanum vulgare teas to treat periodontal disease, including avoiding excessive consumption and potential interactions with other medications, as well as being cautious when ingesting the tea on an empty stomach.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat periodontal disease, a chronic bacterial infection affecting the gums and tooth sockets. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and eliminate the bacterial load, thereby treating the underlying cause of the disease. The bioactive constituents present in Echinacea angustifolia teas, including alkylamides, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid, exhibit potent antioxidant and antibacterial activities that aid in the healing process. Regular consumption of Echinacea angustifolia teas has been found to promote oral health, reduce the risk of periodontal disease, and prevent its progression to more severe conditions.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea angustifolia roots and flowers. Use 1 part roots to 2 parts flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the Echinacea mixture and place it in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Echinacea mixture. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate cup using a strainer or cheesecloth. Discard the solids.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally after meals, for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties to help with periodontal disease.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea angustifolia teas can be used as an alternative treatment for periodontal disease, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its purgative properties. To minimize these side effects, it is recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase the amount while being mindful of your body's response and potential allergic reactions, and to discontinue use if symptoms persist or worsen.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas have been traditionally used to treat periodontal disease due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins present in Silybum marianum teas help to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal disease, thereby promoting healthy gums and preventing tooth loss. The bioactive constituents, including silymarin, isoorientin, and isovitexin, exhibit potent antioxidant and antimicrobial activities that help to combat the underlying causes of periodontal disease. By consuming Silybum marianum teas, individuals can benefit from reduced inflammation, improved oral health, and a lower risk of developing periodontal disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Silybum marianum flowers, 1 cup of boiling water, and a teapot.
Steep the Silybum marianum flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, and stir well.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed for periodontal disease relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to treat periodontal disease, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues, such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken to avoid consuming Silybum marianum teas in excessive amounts, as it may interact with other medications and worsen conditions like bleeding disorders or kidney problems.

intro_study: According to "Iranian journal of basic medical sciences", teas for periodontal disease, specifically green tea, have a large body of evidence supporting their effectiveness in reducing gingival inflammation and bleeding, and improving oral hygiene indices.
